headers: Lookup codes are the list of values for a Lookup Data Type (dropdown) attribute in GRAVTY.
Lookup Code values for Offer Category - Travel, Dining, Entertainment, etc.,
Objective: To provide a comprehensive overview of the Glossary and a step-by-step walkthrough of the discovery questionnaire for GRAVTY Configuration.

Agenda:
1. Glossary
2. Overview of the Workshop Plan
3. Walkthrough of the Discovery Questionnaire

1. Gain familiarity with GRAVTY and LJI-specific terminology.
2. Develop a thorough understanding of the questionnaire's questions and underlying objectives.
Client Business Team, LJI Project Manager, Business Analyst
Please provide the logo of your program or your company's logo in case your program doesn't have one yet.
Please add the cloud drive link for the file here.
Please provide the email addresses (at least one) of the Program Administrators you would like us to invite for GRAVTY loyalty system access.
Please fill in the details here.
Please provide the name of your Loyalty Program.
Please provide the name of the company that owns the Program.

Currency Type 
- For Rewarding Members (Redemption Type)
- For Recognizing Members via Tiers, Gamification Badges, etc. (Recognition Type)
- Partner Program Currency (e.g. AAdvantageMiles in Hilton Honors Program)

Currency to Reward Loyalty Program Members (Member Type Individuals)

Points expire 2 years after the last Member Accrual Transaction, on the Member's Anniversary Date.

- Bonus Points expire in 2 months.
- Happy Hours Points expire in 1 day.
- New Year Points expire on 31st Dec.
Do you expect to receive member transactions in multiple currencies? (Y/N).
Total Sales Influenced by the Offer
The total amount of sales that were influenced by the offer.
The total cost of running the offer.
Do you currently have a data model for managing location-related information?
Which touchpoints/channels will be used for enrolment? Provide an exhaustive list of all the channels through which a member can enroll in your program, e.g., POS, App, Website.
What will be the name of the company hosting the Loyalty Program?
Multi-National Loyalty Program Sponsor Management Related Questions
Refers to Sponsor's additional email address. This would be cc'ed in all of the communication emails to the primary email. This is not visible on the UI and is usually added through merchant portals
Business Contact Details: Latitude of the Business Address
Business Contact Details: Longitude of the Business Address
This will be the Sponsor's name used to identify the Sponsor in GRAVTY.

The visible name attribute can be used to display the Sponsor's brand name on member-facing channels (Mobile, Web, etc.). For example, if you have a Sponsor in multiple countries, in that case, the Sponsor's name can be Burger King Poland and Burger King Austria, but the visible name of the Sponsor can be "Burger King."
Name of the Holding/Parent Company of the Sponsor.
Sponsor specific URL used to enroll members into the program. Additionally, it can be utilized to track the number of members getting enrolled through the Sponsor.
Short Name of the Sponsor. The Sponsor's short name should be unique (No two sponsors in the tenant can have the same Short name) in the program across all the Sponsors.

The Logo of the Sponsor. 
[Files must be 200x200px minimum, and not exceed 2MB, accepted formats PNG, JPG and GIF.]

Header/Banner image of the Sponsor.
[Files must be 200x200px minimum and not exceed 2MB, accepted formats PNG, JPG, and GIF.]
Sponsor facets can be used for optimizing the Sponsor's search and recommendations.

The start date of the Sponsor's validity is the date from which BITs (transactions) are sent to GRAVTY by this sponsor. If past-dated BITs (transactions) are sent from a sponsor, the start date should be determined by the oldest past-dated BIT that would be sent to GRAVTY.
End date of the Sponsor's validity.
Number of Employees in Sponsor's Company/Organization.
Name of the primary sponsor representative. This field is Mandatory only when sponsor verification is needed.
Email of the primary sponsor representative. This field is Mandatory only when sponsor verification is needed.
Phone number of the primary sponsor representative. This field is Mandatory only when sponsor verification is needed.
Latitude of the Sponsor's Headquarters Address.
Longitude of the Sponsor's Headquarters Address.
The operation timings of the sponsor. You can set different open times for different days of the week.
The operation timings of the sponsor. You can set different closing times for different days of the week.
The "Base Rate: Currency" attribute refers to the specific currency against which a base rate is configured.
The "Base Rate: Loyalty Account" attribute refers to the specific loyalty account against which a base rate is configured.
The "Base Rate: Value" attribute refers to the number of points to be rewarded in the respective loyalty account on 1 unit of currency spent.
"Base Rate Validity: Start Date" refers to the commencement date from which the specified base rate becomes applicable.
"Base Rate Validity: End Date" refers to the conclusion date, indicating until when the specified base rate remains valid. Once the end date is reached, the base rate will no longer be applicable.
The "Redemption Threshold: Loyalty Account" attribute refers to the specific loyalty account for which the Redemption Threshold is configured.
The "Redemption Threshold: Value" attribute refers to the maximum number of points that all members can redeem from the respective loyalty account.
Describes Sponsor Location's status, only active locations transactions would be valid
Please provide the name of the Sponsor to which the location belongs.
The location name attribute can be used to show the location's name on member-facing channels such as Mobile, Web, etc.
The type of location, whether it is a store, restaurant, etc.

Please provide the unique code you wish to use for identifying a location in GRAVTY. This location code is sent as part of the BIT (transaction) to GRAVTY. It's important to note that the location code cannot be updated once a location is created.
The phone number for the location that will be visible to members on member-facing channels, such as the app and website.
Sponsor-specific location URL used to enroll members into the program. Additionally, it can be utilized to track the number of members getting enrolled through the location.
Location facets can be used for optimizing location search and recommendations.
Email address of the contact person for location. This person will be registered as a GRAVTY user with the role of Sponsor Location Representative and granted access to this specific location.
Name of the location's contact person. The contact name is mandatory if an email is provided.
Contact number of the contact person.
Indicate whether the location is offline or online. If it is an online location, the domain URL is mandatory; for offline locations, the address is mandatory.
Latitude of the location. This field is mandatory for offline locations.
Longitude of the location. This field is mandatory for offline locations.
The domain URL of the online location. This field is mandatory for online locations.
Refers to the location's destination, for example, the name of the shopping mall, hotel, etc.

Location image of the Sponsor.
[Files must be 200x200px minimum and not exceed 2MB, accepted formats PNG, JPG, and GIF.].
The operation timings of the location. You can set different open times for different days of the week.
The operation timings of the location. You can set different closing times for different days of the week.
Sponsor to which the Product belongs to
Product's Name, visible in GRAVTY and also on the member facing portals
The name of the product that will be displayed in GRAVTY and on member-facing portals such as Mobile App, Website, etc.,
Please specify the product's category, such as Electronics, Apparel, Automotive, etc.
Please specify the product's line, such as BMW 3 Series, Samsung Galaxy Series, Coca-Cola Beverages, etc.,
The cost of the product incurred by the sponsor in the program currency (USD, EUR, MXN, etc.).
The start date of the Product's validity.
The End date of the Product's validity.
The "Base Reward In" attribute refers to the specific loyalty account against which the base reward value will be configured.
The "Base Reward" attribute refers to the number of points to be rewarded in the respective loyalty account for the purchase of 1 unit of SKU.
The Product External ID is a unique identifier for the product or Stock-keeping unit (SKU) that the source system can pass in the BIT (Transaction) when a member purchases SKU(s).
Product facets can be used for optimizing the Product's search and recommendations.
Does your Program have a Tier structure? If Yes, kindly provide Tier related details.
Yes and Starbucks Rewards program has 3 Tiers.

Does a member earn based on the total amount spent, or is it calculated on a line level (each SKU or specific transaction level)? For instance, does the total bill amount determine it, or is it assessed more granularly, such as each line item for Retail, Room Stay, or F&B spent for Hospitality, or each ancillary for airlines?
Is there a minimum criteria within the program that members must meet to earn points? For example, does the bill amount need to exceed $100?
No, bill amount does not need to exceed any minimum amount
Refers to the ID of BIT which is to be cancelled
Name of the Member who referred this activity to another member.
Code of the bank used for transaction.
Reward offers involve the issuance of loyalty points and privileges when a member fulfills a specific call to action.
Upgrade your airline seat for 1000 loyalty points
Award offers allow members to redeem points for a product or service. Members use points to pay for specific services or products.
Member would be able to follow these sponsors through member facing portals and offer would be targetted to these followers
Number of times an offer has been applied for the transactions done by the members
Mention the sponsor under which the members will get enrolled.
Provide a list of user's roles who are going to access GRAVTY in the below table.

Password Expiry Policy: Specify the password expiration period for GRAVTY users (this doesn't apply to loyalty program members). The default setting for this value is 90 days and it should be within the range of 7 to 90 days.
Specify whether the user should have unlimited access or limited access with options such as Hourly limit, Daily limit, or Fixed Date.

This field is mandatory only in the case of limited access for users. Provide the value for "Valid Until/For" based on the access limit type:
- Hourly Limit: Specify the number of hours.
- Daily Limit: Specify the number of days.
- Fixed Date: Provide the end date and time for access.
Specify the relevant GRAVTY role that the user should be assigned.
This field is mandatory only for Sponsor Level users. Please select the sponsor for whom users will require access. In the case of multi-sponsor users, specify the names of multiple sponsors.

This field is mandatory only for Sponsor Location Level users. Please select the sponsor location for whom users will require access. In the case of multi-sponsor location users, specify the names of multiple sponsor locations.
User profile photo. Users can also upload their photo after logging in.
To manually adjust the points for the member.

Summary of the Notification Use Case

Source Systems: What are the source systems from which data will be extracted and migrated into GRAVTY®? Will it be one system or multiple systems? If multiple, then please specify the type of data coming from each source system.
 
E.g., One System from where all the data has to be migrated to GRAVTY (or) Different systems from which data has to be migrated into GRAVTY®.
1. X-System: Member Profile data will be shared from this primary system.
2. EComm System: Online member data , including transactions,will be shared from Ecomm system.
3. POS System: POS Transactions will be shared from the POS system.

Entities List: What are the entities that need to be migrated into GRAVTY®?
1. Member Profile data
2. Member Cards data 
3. Member Transaction data
4. Member Point Balance
5. Member Privileges & Expiry data
6. Retro Transaction data
7. SKU Level data
8. Any other entities

- Member Profile Data
- Member Point Balance
- SKUs Catalogs
- Promotions Catalogs
- Member Transaction Data
Target Systems: What are the DWH systems to which data will be exported from GRAVTY®?

Entities List: What are the entities that need to be exported from GRAVTY®?
 1. Member Profile data
 2. Member Transaction data
 3. Member Point Balances
 4. Member Privileges & Expiry data
 5. Program Offers
 6. Sponsor Products
 7. Any other entities
What is the prepaid reserved balance amount to be set up for the Program?
Prepaid Reserved Balance acts as a escrow balance which will be used once the entire available prepaid balance is completed.
Does the program require to charge additional admin fee on top of the billing rates? If Yes than what is the amount?
Non Mandatory if the toggle is Off. Mandatory If the toggle is On.
How important is it for you to have a system that can store and evaluate membership related details like membership ID, membership name, sponsor associated to the membership, etc. efficiently?
How would you envision using the data stored for member details and FFP members to enhance your business operations?

Retro Request Setup enables users to store and manage retro requests as service requests facilitating efficient tracking and automated fulfilment of Retro Requests.

Airline Redemption Setup gives airline program managers a powerful and precise way to setup redemption rates and award values based on different program parameters. It enables the users to support calculation of redemption pricing dynamically based on flight details, such as the origin and destination airports, fare class, flight dates and any other parameters impacting the redemption price.
